name: Luma AI (Dream Machine API)
what_it_does: AI video generation API (Dream Machine) that creates high-quality 5-second video clips from text prompts or images. Also offers 3D generation from images (Genie) and NeRF-based 3D scene reconstruction. Dream Machine is known for high motion quality and photorealism compared to competitors. REST API for programmatic video generation in agent content pipelines.
use_cases: Generate product demonstration videos from text descriptions for agent-driven e-commerce content pipelines
Create animated clips from static images for agent-managed social media content workflows
Produce short-form video content (ads, reels, marketing clips) programmatically for agent content automation
Generate b-roll footage for agent-produced long-form video content without stock footage licensing
Build agent content pipelines that convert text scripts to video sequences for explainer videos or presentations
not_for: Long-form video (minutes) — Dream Machine generates 5-second clips; not suitable for full-length video
Real-time video generation — rendering takes minutes per clip
Highly controlled, brand-specific visuals — generative video has limited controllability compared to traditional video production
best_when: You need high-quality short-form AI video generation for agent content automation — particularly for creative/marketing content where motion quality matters.
avoid_when: You need long video content, real-time generation, or precise visual control — traditional video production or specialized tools are more appropriate.

auth.notes: API key in Authorization Bearer header. Keys from Luma Labs developer portal.

pricing.paid_tiers: Credits-based: ~$0.0025/second of video generated; standard 5s clip ~$0.01-0.05 depending on resolution
pricing.notes: Per-credit pricing for video generation. Higher resolution and longer clips cost more credits. Credits can be purchased in bundles. Pricing competitive with Runway and Pika.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent generation limits; queue-based async model

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Asynchronous generation model with generation_id for polling. Status endpoint returns 'pending', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed'. Error details indicate prompt rejection or generation failure reasons.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Video generation creates new generation IDs — not idempotent. Agents must track generation IDs to avoid duplicate generations and wasted credits. Status polling is idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Generation is asynchronous and takes 1-3 minutes — agents must poll status with backoff or implement webhook-style polling loop
Generated video URLs expire after 24-72 hours — agents must download and store videos before URL expiry
Prompt adherence for video is less precise than image generation — agent-generated prompts may need iteration to achieve desired results
Credit costs are charged at generation start, not completion — failed generations may still consume credits depending on failure point
Content moderation rejects prompts with NSFW, violence, or specific person references — agent prompts must be filtered before submission

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Content moderation for generated video. Generated content stored in Luma's infrastructure with temporary URLs. No SOC2 publicly confirmed.
